entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A staple crop studied for performance under waterlogged conditions

entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: A sensitive cash crop affected by waterlogging

entities.name: CORN
entities.description: A sensitive staple crop affected by waterlogging

entities.name: JAKUMBÈ
entities.description: A sorghum cultivar (CSM63E) with high grain yield

entities.name: PR3009B
entities.description: A hybrid sorghum cultivar bred for high harvest index

entities.name: GNOFING
entities.description: A local sorghum cultivar selected by farmers for high biomass

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: ABOVE GROUND BIOMASS YIELD
entities.description: The total dry matter produced above the soil surface

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total above ground biomass

entities.name: WATERLOGGING RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to tolerate excessive water in the soil

entities.name: FARMERS-FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: An experimental approach conducted in actual farmers' fields

entities.name: WATERLOGGING
entities.description: A condition where soil is saturated with water, restricting oxygen

entities.name: INTRA-SEASONAL DROUGHTS
entities.description: Temporary periods of water deficit during the growing season

entities.name: PRECIPITATION VARIABILITY
entities.description: Fluctuations in the amount and timing of rainfall

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: A country in West Africa where the study was conducted

entities.name: CINZANA
entities.description: A rural commune and climate-smart village in Mali

entities.name: SUDANO-SAHELIAN ZONE
entities.description: A bioclimatic zone in West Africa

entities.name: CLIMATE-SMART SOLUTIONS
entities.description: Strategies designed to increase productivity and resilience to climate change

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

relationships.description: Performance of sorghum cultivars was monitored under waterlogging conditions
Jakumbè produced significantly higher grain yields than PR3009B
PR3009B was bred for a high harvest index
Gnofing produced significantly higher above ground biomass
Sorghum cultivars were tested using a farmers-field experiment
The study area is in the Sudano-Sahelian Zone of Mali
Research on waterlogging resilience is needed to strengthen food security
Increasing precipitation variability leads to more frequent waterlogging
Waterlogging restricts the productivity of cotton
Waterlogging restricts the productivity of corn
Sorghum cultivars demonstrated resilience to 20 days of waterlogging
